user_title: COO & Global Head of Change Execution, WPS

talking_points.headline: HSBC AGM Locks RoTE 17%+ Through 2028 and Flags Fraud Controls — H2 Is the COO's Proving Ground
talking_points.why_it_matters: Elhedery's shareholder-facing RoTE commitment and Nelson's public flag of the $400 million fraud provision together make cost discipline and risk-control delivery non-negotiable WPS COO obligations through 2028.
bullets: Georges Elhedery, Group CEO of HSBC, confirmed at today's London AGM that HSBC retains all February targets — RoTE of 17%+ for 2026, 2027, and 2028 with upgraded NII guidance of ~$46 billion — a three-year public commitment that WPS must operationally underpin.

Brendan Nelson, Group Chairman of HSBC, told shareholders the bank conducted a thorough review of a $400 million fraud-related Q1 provision and is updating its risk appetite, making operational resilience and internal control delivery a board-visible H2 priority.

Bank-wide wealth balances at end-2025 reached $2.1 trillion (>$1trn in Asia), validating the Asia growth thesis but raising the COO question of whether WPS's operational model can scale at the pace the strategy now publicly demands.

source_links.title: HSBC AGM: Our Growth Strategy Is Delivering
Bloomberg: HSBC Chair on $400M Fraud Provision

talking_points.headline: FIS-Anthropic AML Agent Sets the Industry AI Governance Standard — WPS Must Match Before H2
talking_points.why_it_matters: The FIS-Anthropic partnership establishes a three-part AI governance standard — auditability, data sovereignty, human oversight — that regulators can now reference, requiring WPS to demonstrate equivalent governance before its own AI agents go to production.
bullets: FIS's Financial Crimes AI Agent compresses AML case investigations from days to minutes with BMO and Amalgamated Bank deploying in H2 2026 — this is a production benchmark, not a pilot concept, against which HSBC will now be measured by peers and regulators.

The agent architecture is explicitly governed: client data stays within FIS-controlled infrastructure, every decision is traceable and auditable, and human investigators remain in the review loop — a governance framework WPS should map its own agentic AI programmes against.

Anthropic embedded forward-deployed engineers to co-design and transfer knowledge so FIS can scale agents independently — this partnership construct gives WPS a documented template for structuring AI vendor risk and knowledge-transfer agreements.

source_links.title: FIS: Agentic AI for Banking with Anthropic
Fintech.Global: FIS Taps Anthropic for AML

talking_points.headline: Singapore Banks Sweep Q1 Records: The COO Benchmark Is Growth AND Cost Discipline, Simultaneously
talking_points.why_it_matters: Singapore's big three posted record wealth revenues while maintaining cost discipline in Q1 2026, setting the dual-KPI benchmark — growth and efficiency in parallel — that WPS must deliver in H2 as HSBC's cost commitments come due.
bullets: Asian Private Banker reports Singapore's big three broke all-time wealth fee records in Q1 2026, with DBS fees up 29% — demonstrating that market-leading wealth growth and disciplined operations are not trade-offs but simultaneous deliverables.

UOB reported Q1 wealth AUM of $156 billion alongside S$1.4 billion net profit, while Standard Chartered continued expanding its Singapore private banking team — three competitors operating at record output with cost efficiency intact.

The pattern across Singapore peers mirrors the precise H2 2026 dual-KPI challenge WPS faces: growing the change delivery programme's output while meeting the cost-to-income commitments that Elhedery publicly reaffirmed at today's AGM.

source_links.title: Asian Private Banker: Singapore Big Three Records

HSBC News.title: HSBC AGM: Asia Wealth Tops $1 Trillion, Elhedery Upgrades NII to $46bn
HSBC News.summary: At today's London AGM CEO Elhedery confirmed bank-wide wealth balances of $2.1 trillion (>$1trn in Asia), upgraded 2026 NII guidance to ~$46bn, and reaffirmed RoTE 17%+ targets through 2028 — publicly locking the Asia wealth growth thesis as a three-year shareholder commitment.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Chair: $400M Fraud Provision Had Thorough Review, Risk Appetite Updated
HSBC News.summary: Chairman Brendan Nelson told AGM shareholders HSBC conducted a thorough review of a $400 million fraud-related provision in Q1 2026 and is updating its risk appetite framework, signalling that risk and control infrastructure is an active board-level priority alongside growth.

AI & Technology.title: FIS + Anthropic Launch Financial Crimes AI Agent: AML Investigations Days to Minutes
AI & Technology.summary: FIS announced its Financial Crimes AI Agent built with Anthropic automatically assembles AML evidence across core banking systems and compresses case investigations from days to minutes; BMO and Amalgamated Bank deploy in H2 2026 with a roadmap spanning credit, onboarding, and fraud prevention.

AI & Technology.title: Citi Arms Wealth Advisors with 4 AI Tools to Cut Busywork
AI & Technology.summary: Citi deployed four AI tools across its wealth division — one client-facing and three advisor-facing — designed to eliminate low-value tasks and shrink the gap between data and personalised advice, complementing the previously announced Citi Sky AI advisor backed by Google DeepMind.

AI & Technology.title: Lloyds: 2026 Is the Year of Agentic AI and a New Era for Finance
AI & Technology.summary: Lloyds Banking Group's insights post frames 2026 as the inflection year where AI agents move from pilots to enterprise production, with leading banks operationalising agents across experiences and processes powered by real-time data, cloud-native platforms, and interoperable core systems.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Singapore's Big Three Break Wealth Fee and AUM Records in Q1 2026
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Asian Private Banker reports DBS, OCBC's Bank of Singapore, and UOB all hit all-time highs in wealth income and AUM in Q1 2026, with DBS wealth fees surging 29% and 58% of AUM now in investment products — a market-wide acceleration of Singapore's private banking ecosystem.

Competitor Intelligence.title: UOB Q1 2026: $156bn Wealth AUM Alongside Record S$1.4bn Net Profit
Competitor Intelligence.summary: UOB reported Q1 2026 net profit of S$1.4 billion on record S$3.4 billion revenue, with Privilege Banking and Private Bank AUM reaching $156 billion comprising S$116bn deposits and S$82bn invested assets, demonstrating sustained wealth growth at Singapore's third-largest bank.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Standard Chartered Private Bank Expands Singapore Team for Chinese Wealth
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Standard Chartered Private Bank is expanding its Singapore-based private banking team, targeting the Chinese wealth segment as part of new Head of Global Wealth Management Marc van de Walle's broader APAC and Gulf expansion strategy.
